nsf_profile.name: Ciprian Demeter

awards.title: REU Site: Research Experiences for Undergraduates in Mathematics at Indiana University
program_references: REU SITE-Res Exp for Ugrd Site
REU SITE-Res Exp for Ugrd Site

papers.title: New discretised polynomial expander and incidence estimates
papers.abstract: We present two applications of recent developments in incidence geometry. One is a $\delta$-discretised version of a particular `Elekes--R\'onyai'expander problem. The second application is an incidence estimate addressing the scenario when both tubes, squares and their shadings satisfy non-concentration assumptions.

papers.title: Incidence estimates for quasi-product sets and applications
papers.abstract: We use recent advances in the theory of Furstenberg sets to prove new incidence results of Szemer\'edi--Trotter strength for $\delta$-discretized structures with Cartesian product flavor. We use these results to make progress on a number of problems that include energy estimates and Fourier decay of fractal measures supported on curves, as well as various sum-product-like results governed by fractal dimension.

papers.title: Restriction and decoupling estimates for the hyperbolic paraboloid in $\mathbb{R}^3$
papers.abstract: We prove bilinear $\ell^2$-decoupling and refined bilinear decoupling inequalities for the truncated hyperbolic paraboloid in $\mathbb{R}^3$. As an application, we prove the associated restriction estimate in the range $p>22/7$, matching an earlier result for the elliptic paraboloid.

papers.title: Beyond Canonical Decoupling
papers.venue: Journal of Fourier Analysis and Applications
papers.abstract: We introduce two families of inequalities. Large ensemble decoupling is connected to the continuous restriction phenomenon. Tight decoupling is connected to the discrete Restriction conjecture for the sphere. Our investigation opens new grounds and answers some questions.

papers.title: Level set estimates for the periodic Schr\"odinger maximal function on $\mathbb{T}^1$
papers.abstract: We prove (essentially) sharp $L^4$ level set estimates for the periodic Schr\"odinger maximal operator in a certain range of the cut-off parameter.

papers.title: On the N-set Occupancy Problem
papers.abstract: We explore variants of the following open question: Split [0,1]2\documentclass[12pt]{minimal} \usepackage{amsmath} \usepackage{wasysym} \usepackage{amsfonts} \usepackage{amssymb} \usepackage{amsbsy} \usepackage{mathrsfs} \usepackage{upgreek} \setlength{\oddsidemargin}{-69pt} \begin{document}$$[0,1]^2$$\end{document} into N2\documentclass[12pt]{minimal} \usepackage{amsmath} \usepackage{wasysym} \usepackage{amsfonts} \usepackage{amssymb} \usepackage{amsbsy} \usepackage{mathrsfs} \usepackage{upgreek} \setlength{\oddsidemargin}{-69pt} \begin{document}$$N^2$$\end{document} squares with side length 1/N. Is there a way to select N of these squares such that each line intersects only O(1) of them?

papers.title: L2 to Lp bounds for spectral projectors on the Euclidean two-dimensional torus
papers.venue: Proceedings of the Edinburgh Mathematical Society
papers.abstract: Abstract We consider spectral projectors associated to the Euclidean Laplacian on the two-dimensional torus, in the case where the spectral window is narrow. Bounds for their L2 to Lp operator norm are derived, extending the classical result of Sogge; a new question on the convolution kernel of the projector is introduced. The methods employed include $\ell^2$ decoupling, small cap decoupling and estimates of exponential sums.

papers.title: Maximal $\Lambda(p)$-subsets of manifolds
papers.abstract: We construct maximal $\Lambda(p)$-subsets on a large class of curved manifolds, in an optimal range of Lebesgue exponents $p$. Our arguments combine restriction estimates and decoupling with old and new probabilistic estimates.

papers.title: Szemer\'edi-Trotter bounds for tubes and applications
papers.abstract: We prove sharp estimates for incidences involving planar tubes that satisfy packing conditions. We apply them to improve the estimates for the Fourier transform of fractal measures supported on planar curves.

papers.title: $L^2$ to $L^p$ bounds for spectral projectors on the Euclidean two-dimensional torus
papers.abstract: We consider spectral projectors associated to the Euclidean Laplacian on the two-dimensional torus, in the case where the spectral window is narrow. Bounds for their L2 to Lp operator norm are derived, extending the classical result of Sogge; a new question on the convolution kernel of the projector is introduced. The methods employed include l2 decoupling, small cap decoupling, and estimates of exponential sums.

papers.title: DECOUPLING FOR CURVED FROSTMAN MEASURES
Fourier decay for curved Frostman measures
papers.abstract: We investigate decoupling for Frostman measures supported on curves with nonzero curvature. We combine this tool with known lower bounds for Furstenberg sets to reprove Orponen's recent result for the parabola.

papers.title: Bourgain’s work in Fourier restriction
papers.venue: Bulletin of the American Mathematical Society
papers.abstract: . This note will show how Bourgain’s work in Fourier restriction theory has built bridges to number theory, partial diﬀerential equations, and additive combinatorics.

papers.title: On restriction of exponential sums to hypersurfaces with zero curvature
papers.abstract: We prove essentially sharp bounds for the $$L^p$$ L p restriction of weighted Gauss sums to monomial curves. Getting the $$L^2$$ L 2 upper bound combines the $$TT^*$$ T T ∗ method for matrices with the first and second derivative test for exponential sums. The matching lower bound follows via constructive interference on short blocks of integers, near the critical point of the phase function. This method is used to make the broader point that restriction to hypersurfaces is really sensitive to curvature. Our results here complement those in [ 4 ].

papers.title: Restriction of Exponential Sums to Hypersurfaces
papers.abstract: We prove moment inequalities for exponential sums with respect to singular measures, whose Fourier decay matches those of curved hypersurfaces. Our emphasis will be on proving estimates that are sharp with respect to the scale parameter $N$, apart from $N^\epsilon $ losses. In a few instances, we manage to remove these losses.

papers.title:
Sharp 
              
                
              
              $$\ell ^p$$
              
                
                  ℓ
                  p
                
              
            -Improving Estimates for the Discrete Paraboloid
On $L^{12}$ square root cancellation for exponential sums associated with nondegenerate curves in ${\mathbb R}^4$
papers.abstract: Abstract. We prove sharp L estimates for exponential sums associated with nondegenerate curves in R. We place Bourgain’s seminal result [2] in a larger framework that contains a continuum of estimates of different flavor. We enlarge the spectrum of methods by combining decoupling with quadratic Weyl sum estimates, to address new cases of interest. All results are proved in the general framework of real analytic curves.

papers.title: BOURGAIN’S WORK IN
An Introduction to Decoupling
The Bourgain–Guth Method
Parabolic Rescaling and a Bilinear-to-Linear Reduction
The Polynomial Method
Decouplings for Other Manifolds
The failure of the fractal uncertainty principle for the Walsh--Fourier transform
papers.abstract: We construct $\delta$-regular sets with $\delta\ge \frac12$ for which the analog of the Bourgain--Dyatlov Fractal Uncertainty Principle fails for the Walsh--Fourier transform.

papers.title: Bilinear Restriction Theory
On the refined Strichartz estimates
papers.abstract: We present a slightly simpler proof of the multilinear refined Strichartz estimate, and prove a slightly more general linear refined Strichartz estimate. Our arguments seek to clarify the connection between these estimates, refined decoupling and tube incidences.

papers.title: Multilinear Kakeya and Restriction Inequalities
Applications of Decoupling
Fourier Restriction, Decoupling, and Applications
papers.abstract: The last fifteen years have seen a flurry of exciting developments in Fourier restriction theory, leading to significant new applications in diverse fields. This timely text brings the reader from the classical results to state-of-the-art advances in multilinear restriction theory, the Bourgain–Guth induction on scales and the polynomial method. Also discussed in the second part are decoupling for curved manifolds and a wide variety of applications in geometric analysis, PDEs (Strichartz estimates on tori, local smoothing for the wave equation) and number theory (exponential sum estimates and the proof of the Main Conjecture for Vinogradov's Mean Value Theorem). More than 100 exercises in the text help reinforce these important but often difficult ideas, making it suitable for graduate students as well as specialists. Written by an author at the forefront of the modern theory, this book will be of interest to everybody working in harmonic analysis.

papers.title: Kakeya and Square Function Estimates
Sharp $l^p$-improving estimates for the discrete paraboloid
papers.abstract: We prove $l^p$-improving estimates for the averaging operator along the discrete paraboloid in the sharp range of $p$ in all dimensions $n\ge 2$.

papers.title: Decoupling for the Moment Curve
Decoupling for the Elliptic Paraboloid
DECOUPLINGS AND APPLICATIONS
papers.abstract: Abstract I will explain a few Fourier analytic results in the spirit of the Littlewood-Paley theorem, when curvature replaces lacunarity. I will explore a wide range of consequences in number theory (exponential sums, Diophantine inequalities, the Lindelof hypothesis) and PDEs (local smoothing for the wave equation, Strichartz estimates on the torus). This talk is purely expository, no proofs, intended for a general audience. Joint with Jean Bourgain.

papers.title: Small cap decouplings
papers.abstract: We develop a toolbox for proving decouplings into boxes with diameter smaller than the canonical scale. As an application of this new technique, we solve three problems for which earlier methods have failed. We start by verifying the small cap decoupling for the parabola. Then we find sharp estimates for exponential sums with small frequency separation on the moment curve in R3\documentclass[12pt]{minimal} \usepackage{amsmath} \usepackage{wasysym} \usepackage{amsfonts} \usepackage{amssymb} \usepackage{amsbsy} \usepackage{mathrsfs} \usepackage{upgreek} \setlength{\oddsidemargin}{-69pt} \begin{document}$$\mathbb {R}^3$$\end{document}. This part of the work relies on recent improved Kakeya-type estimates for planar tubes, as well as on new multilinear incidence bounds for plates and planks. We also combine our method with the recent advance on the reverse square function estimate, in order to prove small cap decoupling into square-like caps for the two dimensional cone. The Appendix by Roger Heath-Brown contains an application of the new exponential sum estimates for the moment curve, to the Riemann zeta-function.

papers.title: Background and Notation
Decouplings for Real Analytic Surfaces of Revolution
papers.abstract: We extend the decoupling results of the first two authors to the case of real analytic surfaces of revolution in \({\mathbb R}^3\). New examples of interest include the torus and the perturbed cone.

papers.title: On the number of $k$th powers inside arithmetic progressions
papers.abstract: We find upper bounds that are sharp for the number of $k$th powers inside arbitrary arithmetic progressions whose step has $O(1)$ many divisors.

papers.title: Three Applications of the Siegel Mass Formula
papers.abstract: We present three applications of the Siegel mass formula. First we estimate the number of solutions of a quadratic system of equations. We also include estimates for the distribution of lattice points on caps of four dimensional spheres and for the number of non-congruent lattice tetrahedra.

papers.title: A decoupling for Cantor-like sets
papers.venue: Proceedings of the American Mathematical Society
papers.abstract: We consider partitions of the parabola determined by Cantor-like sets and prove decouplings in the range 2 ≤ p ≤ 6 that are independent of the parameters defining these sets. In the process, we further clarify and simplify the argument from [2]. 1. The main conjecture and results Consider the n− 1 dimensional unit sphere Sn−1 and truncated paraboloid Pn−1 = {(ξ1, . . . , ξn−1, ξ 1 + . . .+ ξ n−1) : |ξi| ≤ 1}, equipped with surface measure dσ. The following decoupling theorem was proved in [2]. Theorem 1.1. If S is either Pn−1 or Sn−1 then for each 2 ≤ p ≤ 2(n+1) n−1 , f : S → C, > 0 and R ≥ δ−2 and for each finitely overlapping cover of S with caps of diameter ∼ δ ‖f̂dσ‖Lp(wBR ) . δ − ( ∑ τ⊂S: δ−cap ‖f̂τdσ‖2Lp(wBR )) 1/2 with the implicit constant independent of R. Here and in the following, BR will refer to an arbitrary cube in R with side length R and center c, and wBR(x) is a weight of the form (1 + |x−c| R )−C , for some unspecified C. This implies rather immediately the following exponential sum estimate. Theorem 1.2. For each cap τ in the cover of S, let ξτ ∈ τ . Then for 2 ≤ p ≤ 2(n+1) n−1 and R ≥ δ−2 |BR|‖ ∑ τ e(ξτ · x)‖Lp(BR) . δ− + n−1 2 . Define for k ≥ 2 the additive energy of Λ ⊂ S Ek(Λ) = |{(λ1, . . . , λ2k) ∈ Λ : λ1 + . . .+ λk = λk+1 + . . .+ λ2k}|. Letting R → ∞ when n = 2, p = 6 and also when n = 3, p = 4 in Theorem 1.2 we get the following estimate for additive energies (see [2]). For each δ-separated set Λ on P or S we have E3(Λ) . δ− |Λ|, and for each δ-separated set Λ on P or S we have E2(Λ) . δ− |Λ|. It is natural to ask whether the “correct” analogues of these inequalities hold in the absence of quantitative separation.

papers.title: Sharp decouplings for three dimensional manifolds in R 5
On the restriction theorem for the paraboloid in ${\mathbb R}^4$
papers.abstract: We prove that recent breaking by Zahl of the $\frac32$ barrier in Wolff's estimate on the Kakeya maximal operator in $\mathbb R^4$ leads to improving the $\frac{14}{5}$ threshold for the restriction problem for the paraboloid in $\mathbb R^4$. One of the ingredients is a new trilinear estimate. The proofs are deliberately presented in a nontechnical and concise format, so as to make the arguments more readable and focus attention on the key tools.

papers.title: A study guide for the l2 decoupling theorem
papers.venue: Chinese Annals of Mathematics. Series B

papers.title: A few thoughts on the polynomial method
Decouplings for curves and hypersurfaces with nonzero Gaussian curvature
A study guide for the $l^2$ Decoupling Theorem
Schrödinger maximal function estimates via the pseudoconformal transformation
papers.abstract: We present an alternative way to recover the recent result from \cite{LR} using the pseudoconformal transformation.

papers.title: Sharp bounds for the cubic Parsell-Vinogradov system in two dimensions
papers.abstract: We prove a sharp decoupling for a certain two dimensional surface in R^9. As an application, we obtain the full range of expected estimates for the cubic Parsell-Vinogradov system in two dimensions.

papers.title: A study guide for the l2 decoupling theorem
papers.abstract: This paper contains a detailed, self contained and more streamlined proof of the l2 decoupling theorem for hypersurfaces from the paper of Bourgain and Demeter in 2015. The authors hope this will serve as a good warm up for the readers interested in understanding the proof of Vinogradov’s mean value theorem from the paper of Bourgain, Demeter and Guth in 2015.

papers.title: Sharp decouplings for three dimensional manifolds in $\mathbb R^5$
papers.abstract: We prove a sharp decoupling for a class of three dimensional manifolds in R.

papers.title: Mean value estimates for Weyl sums in two dimensions
papers.venue: Journal of the London Mathematical Society
papers.abstract: We use decoupling theory to estimate the number of solutions for quadratic and cubic Parsell–Vinogradov systems in two dimensions.

papers.title: The proof of the l 2 Decoupling Conjecture
Decouplings for surfaces in R^4
Optimal mean value estimates for the quadratic Weyl sums in two dimensions
papers.abstract: We use decoupling theory to prove a sharp (up to $N^\epsilon$ losses) estimate for Vinogradov's mean value theorem in two dimensions

papers.title: Proof of the main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem for degrees higher than three
papers.abstract: We prove the main conjecture in Vinogradov's Mean Value Theorem for degrees higher than three. This will be a consequence of a sharp decoupling inequality for curves

papers.title: Book Review: Classical and multilinear harmonic analysis, Volume 1; Classical and multilinear harmonic analysis, Volume 2
New Bounds for the Discrete Fourier Restriction to the Sphere in 4D and 5D
Incidence theory and restriction estimates
papers.abstract: We investigate the interplay between the discrete restriction phenomenon and incidence theory. Two angles are explored. One is a refinement of the machinery developed by Thomas Wolff, which when combined with a recent subcritical estimate of Bourgain leads to new Strichartz estimates for irrational tori. The other one connects various additive energies with the Szemer\'edi-Trotter Theorem. The combination of the two approaches recovers the best known Strichartz estimates for the classical torus in dimensions $n\ge 3$, without any appeal to number theory.

papers.title: C A ] 9 J an 2 01 4 INCIDENCE THEORY AND DISCRETE RESTRICTION ESTIMATES
$l^p$ decouplings for hypersurfaces with nonzero Gaussian curvature
papers.abstract: We prove an $l^p$ decoupling inequality for hypersurfaces with nonzero Gaussian curvature and use it to derive a corresponding $l^p$ decoupling for curves not contained in a hyperplane. This extends our earlier work from [2]

papers.title: Decouplings for curves and hypersurfaces with nonzero Gaussian curvature
papers.abstract: We prove two types of results. First we develop the decoupling theory for hypersurfaces with nonzero Gaussian curvature, which extends our earlier work from [4]. As a consequence of this, we obtain sharp (up to ε losses) Strichartz estimates for the hyperbolic Schrödinger equation on the torus. Our second main result is an l2 decoupling for nondegenerate curves, which has implications for Vinogradov’s mean value theorem.

papers.title: DECOUPLINGS FOR CURVES
New bounds for the discrete Fourier restriction to the sphere in four and five dimensions
papers.abstract: We improve the range for the discrete Fourier restriction to the four and five dimensional spheres. We rely on two new ingredients, incidence theory and Siegel's mass formula.

papers.title: On the number of non-congruent lattice tetrahedra
papers.abstract: We prove that there are "many" non-congruent tetrahedra in the truncated lattice $[0,q]^3\cap \mathbb{Z}^3$.

papers.title: Endpoint Bounds for the Quartile Operator
papers.venue: Journal of Fourier Analysis and Applications

papers.title: Some new light on a few classical results
papers.abstract: The purpose of this paper is to describe a unified approach to proving vector-valued inequalities without relying on the full strength of weighted theory. Our applications include the Fefferman–Stein and Cordoba–Fefferman inequalities, as well as the vector-valued Carleson operator. Using this approach we also produce a proof of the boundedness of the classical bi-parameter multiplier operators, which does not rely on product theory. Our arguments are inspired by the vector-valued restricted type interpolation used by Bateman and Thiele (2013).

papers.title: Endpoint Bounds for the Quartile Operator
papers.abstract: It is a result by Lacey and Thiele (Ann. of Math. (2) 146(3):693–724, 1997; ibid. 149(2):475–496, 1999) that the bilinear Hilbert transform maps $L^{p_{1}}(\mathbb{R}) \times L^{p_{2}}(\mathbb{R}) $ into $L^{p_{3}}(\mathbb{R})$ whenever (p1,p2,p3) is a Hölder tuple with p1,p2>1 and $p_{3}>\frac{2}{3}$. We study the behavior of the quartile operator, which is the Walsh model for the bilinear Hilbert transform, when $p_{3}=\frac{2}{3}$. We show that the quartile operator maps $L^{p_{1}}(\mathbb{R}) \times L^{p_{2}}(\mathbb{R}) $ into $L^{\frac{2}{3},\infty}(\mathbb{R})$ when p1,p2>1 and one component is restricted to subindicator functions. As a corollary, we derive that the quartile operator maps $L^{p_{1}}(\mathbb{R}) \times L^{p_{2},\frac{2}{3}}(\mathbb{R}) $ into $L^{\frac{2}{3},\infty}(\mathbb{R})$. We also provide weak type estimates and boundedness on Orlicz-Lorentz spaces near p1=1,p2=2 which improve, in the Walsh case, the results of Bilyk and Grafakos (J. Geom. Anal. 16 (4):563–584, 2006) and Carro et al. (J. Math. Anal. Appl. 357(2):479–497, 2009). Our main tool is the multi-frequency Calderón-Zygmund decomposition from (Nazarov et al. in Math. Res. Lett. 17(3):529–545, 2010).

papers.title: Improved estimates for the discrete Fourier restriction to the higher dimensional sphere
papers.abstract: We improve the exponent for the discrete Fourier restriction to the $n$ dimensional sphere, from $p=\frac{2(n+1)}{n-3}$ to $p=\frac{2n}{n-3}$, when $n\ge 4$.

papers.title: Bilinear Fourier Restriction Theorems
papers.venue: Journal of Fourier Analysis and Applications

papers.title: Logarithmic Lp Bounds for Maximal Directional Singular Integrals in the Plane
Logarithmic Lp Bounds for Maximal Directional Singular Integrals in the Plane
papers.abstract:
AbstractLet K be a Calderón–Zygmund convolution kernel on ℝ. We discuss the Lp-boundedness of the maximal directional singular integral 
$$T_{\mathbf{V}} f (x)= \sup_{v \in \mathbf{V}} \bigg| \int_{\mathbb{R}} f(x+t v) K(t) \, \mathrm{d} {t} \bigg| $$ where V is a finite set of N directions. Logarithmic bounds (for 2≤p<∞) are established for a set V of arbitrary structure. Sharp bounds are proved for lacunary and Vargas sets of directions. The latter include the case of uniformly distributed directions and the finite truncations of the Cantor set.We make use of both classical harmonic analysis methods and product-BMO based time-frequency analysis techniques. As a further application of the latter, we derive an Lp almost orthogonality principle for Fourier restrictions to cones.

papers.title: Bilinear Fourier Restriction Theorems
papers.abstract: We provide a general scheme for proving Lp estimates for certain bilinear Fourier restrictions outside the local L2 setting. As an application, we show how such estimates follow for the lacunary polygon. In contrast with prior approaches, our argument avoids any use of the Rubio de Francia Littlewood–Paley inequality.

papers.title: A guide to Carleson's Theorem
papers.abstract: This paper is meant to be a gentle introduction to Carleson's Theorem on pointwise convergence of Fourier series.

papers.title: L2 bounds for a Kakeya‐type maximal operator in ℝ3
papers.abstract: We prove that the maximal operator obtained by taking averages at scale 1 along N arbitrary directions on the sphere is bounded in L2(R3) by N1/4log N. When the directions are N−1/2 separated, we improve the bound to N1/4log N . Apart from the logarithmic terms these bounds are optimal.

papers.title: Linear independence of time frequency translates for special configurations
papers.abstract: We prove that for any 4 points in the plane that belong to 2 parallel lines, there is no linear dependence between the associated time-frequency translates of any nontrivial Schwartz function. If mild Diophantine properties are satisfied, we also prove linear independence in the category of $L^2(\R)$ functions.

papers.title: Singular integrals along $N$ directions in $\mathbb{R}^{2}$
On the finite linear independence of lattice Gabor systems
papers.abstract: In the restricted setting of product phase space lattices, we give an alternate proof of P. Linnell's theorem on the finite linear independence of lattice Gabor systems in $L^2(\mathbb R^d)$. Our proof is based on a simple argument from the spectral theory of random Schr\"odinger operators; in the one-dimensional setting, we recover the full strength of Linnell's result for general lattices.

papers.title: Proof of the HRT conjecture for (2,2) configurations
On the two-dimensional bilinear Hilbert transform
Singular Integrals Along N Directions in R^2
papers.abstract: We prove optimal bounds in L^2(R^2) for the maximal oper- ator obtained by taking a singular integral along N arbitrary directions in the plane. We also give a new proof for the optimal L^2 bound for the single scale Kakeya maximal function.

papers.title: On some maximal multipliers in $L^p$
papers.abstract: We extend an $L^2$ maximal multiplier result of Bourgain to all $L^p$ spaces, $1<p<\infty$.

papers.title: ON SOME MAXIMAL MULTIPLIERS IN L p CIPRIAN DEMETER
2 CIPRIAN DEMETER AND CHRISTOPH THIELE
Multilinear singular operators with fractional rank
papers.abstract: We prove bounds for multilinear operators on $\R^d$ given by multipliers which are singular along a $k$ dimensional subspace. The new case of interest is when the rank $k/d$ is not an integer. Connections with the concept of {\em true complexity} from Additive Combinatorics are also investigated.

papers.title: Improved Range in the Return Times Theorem
papers.abstract: Abstract We prove that the Return Times Theoremholds true for pairs of ${{L}^{p}}\,-\,{{L}^{q}}$ functions, whenever $\frac{1}{p}\,+\,\frac{1}{q}\,<\,\frac{3}{2}$ .

papers.title: On the two dimensional Bilinear Hilbert Transform
papers.abstract: We investigate the Bilinear Hilbert Transform in the plane and the pointwise convergence of bilinear averages in Ergodic theory, arising from $\Z^2$ actions. Our techniques combine novel one and a half dimensional phase-space analysis with more standard one dimensional theory.

papers.title: {Spaces of Infinite Measure and Pointwise Convergence of the Bilinear Hilbert and Ergodic Averages Defined by $L^{p}$-Isometries
papers.abstract: We generalize the respective ``double recurrence'' results of Bourgain and of the second author, which established for pairs of $L^{\infty}$ functions on a finite measure space the a.e. convergence of the discrete bilinear ergodic averages and of the discrete bilinear Hilbert averages defined by invertible measure-preserving point transformations. Our generalizations are set in the context of arbitrary sigma-finite measure spaces and take the form of a.e. convergence of such discrete averages, as well as of their continuous variable counterparts, when these averages are defined by Lebesgue space isometries and act on $L^{p_{1}}\times L^{p_{2}}$ ($ 1<p_{1},p_{2}<\infty $, $p_{1}^{-1}+p_{2}^{-1}<3/2$). In the setting of an arbitrary measure space, this yields the a.e. convergence of these discrete bilinear averages when they act on $L^{p_{1}}\times L^{p_{2}}$ and are defined by an invertible measure-preserving point transformation.

papers.title: Divergence of combinatorial averages and the unboundedness of the trilinear Hilbert transform
papers.abstract: Abstract We consider multilinear averages in ergodic theory and harmonic analysis and prove their divergence in some range of Lp spaces. This contrasts with the positive behavior exhibited by these averages in a different range, as proved in Demeter et al [Maximal multilinear operators. Trans. Amer. Math. Soc.360(9) (2008), 4989–5042]. We also prove that the trilinear Hilbert transform is unbounded in a similar range of Lp spaces. The principle underlying these constructions is stated, setting the stage for more general results.

papers.title: Modulation invariant bilinear T(1) theorem
papers.abstract: We prove a T(1) theorem for bilinear singular integral operators (trilinear forms) with a one-dimensional modulation symmetry.

papers.title: The Walsh model for $M_2^*$ Carleson
papers.abstract: We study the Walsh model of a certain maximal truncation of Carleson's operator, related to the Return Times Theorem from Ergodic Theory.

papers.title: Pointwise convergence of the ergodic bilinear Hilbert transform
papers.abstract: Let ${\bf X}=(X, \Sigma, m, \tau)$ be a dynamical system. We prove that the bilinear series $\sideset{}{'}\sum_{n=-N}^{N}\frac{f(\tau^nx)g(\tau^{-n}x)}{n}$ converges almost everywhere for each $f,g\in L^{\infty}(X).$ We also give a proof along the same lines of Bourgain's analog result for averages.

papers.title: Breaking the duality in the return times theorem
papers.abstract: We prove Bourgain's Return Times Theorem for a range of exponents $p$ and $q$ that are outside the duality range. An oscillation result is used to prove hitherto unknown almost everywhere convergence for the signed average analog of Bourgain's averages. As an immediate corollary we obtain a Wiener-Wintner type of result for the ergodic Hilbert series.

papers.title: Analysis and Ergodic Theory Summer School , Lake Arrowhead
Randomly weighted series of iid's in $L^1$
Maximal multilinear operators
papers.abstract: We establish multilinear L P bounds for a class of maximal multilinear averages of functions of one variable, reproving and generalizing the bilinear maximal function bounds of Lacey (2000). As an application we obtain almost everywhere convergence results for these averages, and in some cases we also obtain almost everywhere convergence for their ergodic counterparts on a dynamical system.

papers.title: Almost everywhere convergence of series in L^1
ALMOST EVERYWHERE CONVERGENCE OF SERIES IN L
The Best Constants Associated with some Weak Maximal Inequalities in Ergodic Theory
papers.venue: Canadian Journal of Mathematics - Journal Canadien de Mathematiques

papers.title: Qualitative and Quantitative Analysis of Weighted Ergodic Theorems
Weak-L 1 estimates and ergodic theorems
Another Collinearity Result for Triangles: 10845
Sharpley's Bottom Historic Sites: Phase I Interdisciplinary Investigations, Tombigbee River Multi-Resource District, Alabama and Mississippi
Phase I Interdisciplinary Investigations at Sharpley's Bottom Historic Sites, Tombigbee River Multiresource District, Alabama and Mississippi
